dataset: A large-scale dataset of pre- and postsurgical MRI data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (OpenNeuro ds005713)
resolution_method: OpenAlex DOI lookup resolved to W4417304259; the OpenAlex cites: filter (works?filter=cites:W4417304259) succeeded on re-query and returned 4 citing works directly. This was cross-checked against an earlier-session fallback (Semantic Scholar citation graph + Europe PMC full-text search for the literal accession string "ds005713", used when OpenAlex list endpoints were rate-limited) which had surfaced 5 genuine-reuse papers via full-text accession matching. Merging both passes: 3 works are confirmed by both methods, 2 works were found only by the earlier full-text/Semantic-Scholar fallback (not yet reflected in OpenAlex's citation index -- citation-indexing lag), and 1 additional work was found only by the direct OpenAlex cites: filter (not yet indexed by Europe PMC/Semantic Scholar at the time of the earlier pass). Total distinct genuine-reuse works across both passes: 6.
citing_works_note: Across the two resolution passes, 6 distinct papers show clear evidence of actually reusing ds005713 MRI/clinical data (either by explicit accession-string citation in full text, or by cohort/method description matching the dataset's pre/post-surgical TN design). One additional citing work (a companion olfactory-groove-meningioma data descriptor by the same Novosibirsk group, cited via OpenAlex's cites: filter) cites the TN data descriptor only as a methodological/BIDS-formatting precedent and does NOT reuse ds005713 data -- excluded, see excluded_as_non_reuse.
dominant_tasks: cross-condition specificity (comparator cohort use, one instance)
methods_used: diffusion tensor imaging / fixel-based analysis (white matter microstructure)
structure-function coupling via graph harmonic modeling
network control theory / structural connectome controllability
imaging-transcriptomics (Allen Human Brain Atlas + PLS regression with spin-test permutation)
imaging-genetics integration with whole-genome sequencing / GWAS-derived SNPs

key_publications.title: Transcriptional correlates of structure-function coupling plasticity in trigeminal neuralgia: unveiling the synaptic and metabolic associations
key_publications.authors: Zhang J, Nie X, Fu S, Lu Y, Liu T, Zhang Z, Wu J, Tong X, Yan T
key_publications.method: Longitudinal multimodal MRI; graph harmonic model for structure-function coupling; PLS regression against Allen Human Brain Atlas transcriptomic data
key_publications.n_used: 87 TN patients (pre/post-treatment subset), 42 healthy controls, drawn from ds005713 (explicitly notes upgrading from v1.0.1 to v2.0.1 for added clinical endpoints)
key_publications.finding: Reduced global structure-function coupling in TN normalizes after surgical treatment; postoperative reorganization correlates with pain reduction; coupling changes spatially linked to synaptic/opioid-signaling and metabolic gene expression

key_publications.title: No Glymphatic Dysfunction in Trigeminal Neuralgia: A Preliminary Study
key_publications.method: DTI-ALPS (diffusion tensor image analysis along the perivascular space) index computed on diffusion MRI
key_publications.n_used: Subset of ds005713 patients vs. healthy controls, pre- and post-decompression surgery
key_publications.finding: No significant glymphatic function differences between TN patients and controls, nor pre- vs. post-surgery

key_publications.title: Cerebellar neurocan gene expression is a common pathogenic factor of trigeminal neuralgia and depression
key_publications.method: Integration of neuroimaging (ds005713 MRI) with whole-genome sequencing / SNP-based gene-tissue association analysis
key_publications.finding: Cerebellar NCAN gene expression implicated as shared pathogenic factor linking TN and depression

key_publications.title: Fixel-based analysis reveals distributed white matter alterations associated with pain severity in trigeminal neuralgia
key_publications.authors: Chu T, Xiong F, Gao F, Tian X, Tu Y
key_publications.method: Fixel-based analysis (fibre density, fibre-bundle cross-section, FDC) with connectivity-based fixel enhancement, FWE-corrected
key_publications.n_used: 65 TN patients, 42 healthy controls (subset of ds005713)
key_publications.finding: Distributed, tract-specific white matter alterations beyond trigeminal pathways (corticospinal tract, cerebellar peduncles); macrostructural bundle measures correlate with pain severity

key_publications.title: Network control theory uncovers aberrant connectome controllability in trigeminal neuralgia
key_publications.method: Diffusion tractography, Brainnetome-parcellated structural connectomes, network control theory metrics
key_publications.n_used: 82 TN patients, 42 healthy controls (subset of ds005713)
key_publications.finding: Aberrant structural network controllability in TN relative to controls

key_publications.title: Anterior-superior hypothalamic enlargement as specific marker in episodic migraine: converging evidence from an independent discovery-replication design
key_publications.method: Atlas-based automated hypothalamic subunit segmentation on structural MRI; MANOVA and Welch t-tests across two migraine discovery/replication cohorts plus two independent 'chronic pain' comparator cohorts
key_publications.n_used: 119 TN patients, 55 healthy controls (near-complete ds005713 cohort), used as one of two chronic-pain comparator datasets alongside a fibromyalgia cohort
key_publications.finding: No significant hypothalamic subunit volumetric differences detected in the TN cohort, used as evidence that a left anterior-superior hypothalamic enlargement found in migraine is migraine-specific rather than a generic chronic-pain effect

excluded_as_non_reuse.title: Multimodal brain MRI and clinical data in olfactory groove meningioma: a prospective data report
excluded_as_non_reuse.reason: Same Novosibirsk author group (Filimonova, Pashkov, Moysak, Martirosyan, Kurilov, Rzaev) citing their own prior TN data descriptor as a methodological/BIDS-formatting precedent for a NEW, unrelated dataset (olfactory groove meningioma) -- not an analysis performed on ds005713 itself.

saturation_rationale: The data descriptor was published 2025-12-12 (~7 months before this analysis), yet 6 independent secondary-analysis papers already exist -- unusually fast uptake for a niche clinical MRI dataset. However, all 6 cluster tightly around the same class of task: univariate/graph-theoretic neuroimaging biomarker discovery (diffusion microstructure, structure-function coupling, connectome controllability, glymphatic index, imaging-genetics) comparing TN patients vs. controls or pre- vs. post-surgery, plus one instance of the TN cohort being borrowed purely as a cross-condition specificity comparator for an unrelated migraine study. No one has yet used ds005713 for supervised outcome-prediction modeling (e.g., predicting surgical pain relief from baseline imaging), lesion/nerve segmentation, TN subtyping, causal/treatment-effect estimation, formal external validation of a classifier trained elsewhere, or foundation-model fine-tuning. So: the 'easy' descriptive/biomarker comparisons are being harvested quickly (borderline high there, and multiple groups are now drawing on it as a convenient chronic-pain comparator cohort), but the higher-value ML/predictive/segmentation white space is still completely open, which keeps overall saturation at medium rather than high.
